records.ety: [F., the same word as jargon. See Jargon a variety of zircon.]

defs: A mineral consisting predominantly of zirconium silicate (Zr2SiO4) occurring in tetragonal crystals, usually of a brown or gray color. It consists of silica and zirconia. A red variety, used as a gem, is called hyacinth. Colorless, pale-yellow or smoky-brown varieties from Ceylon are called jargon.
an imitation gemstone made of cubic zirconia.
